title: The Palace of Citizens (EADF2026)
synopsis:
As economic crises deepen the gulf between politicians and the public, filmmaker Rui Pires spends a year embedded inside the Portuguese Parliament. Focusing on high-stakes debates over the Basic Housing Law, this documentary opens an unprecedented window into a political black box. Infused with irony and drama, the film captures the complex, often contradictory machinery of governance, framing the parliament as a "Palace of Citizens" to pose a sharp, uncomfortable question about the very essence of modern democracy.

 

MAX Award, Doclisboa International Film Festival
Uruguay International Film Festival

synopsis_lang.en:
As economic crises deepen the gulf between politicians and the public, filmmaker Rui Pires spends a year embedded inside the Portuguese Parliament. Focusing on high-stakes debates over the Basic Housing Law, this documentary opens an unprecedented window into a political black box. Infused with irony and drama, the film captures the complex, often contradictory machinery of governance, framing the parliament as a "Palace of Citizens" to pose a sharp, uncomfortable question about the very essence of modern democracy.

 

MAX Award, Doclisboa International Film Festival
Uruguay International Film Festival
synopsis_lang.zh_hk:
當經濟危機撕裂社會，政客與人民之間的距離，早已演變成各說各話的信心危機。導演深入葡萄牙國會一年，記錄攸關民生的《基本住屋法》角力，揭開這個權力黑盒的神秘面紗，以既諷刺又具戲劇性的視角，直擊議員如何在議事廳內唇槍舌劍與利益博弈，將大眾的切實期望轉化為法律條文。在複雜矛盾的管治機器中，電影將國會描述為一座人民宮殿，拋出尖銳而令人苦思的詰問：這座宮殿究竟是在回應人民切身訴求，還是早已淪為高高在上的民主幻象？

 

里斯本國際紀錄片電影節 葡萄牙競賽最佳電影獎
烏拉圭國際電影節

subtitle: English
